commentaries.author: Aquinas Study Bible
commentaries.content: two breasts: these are preachers from two different peoples, Jews and Gentiles. They are called breasts because they give spiritual milk to the unlearned (St. Bede)

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Song of Songs 4:5 Your two breasts are like two fawns, twins of a gazelle, which feed among the lilies. This comparison does not seem happy: but exactitude is not required. (Calmet) Indeed if we were to take all in the literal sense, a very grotesque figure would arise, with a head like Carmel, a nose like a tower, which shows that the tropological or allegorical sense must be adopted. (Du Hamel) The two Testaments given for our instruction, (chap. ) or the charity towards God and our neighbour, may be meant. (Theodoret).

commentaries.author: Gregory The Dialogist
commentaries.content: In order to [gain knowledge of heavenly things], we study the examples of the saints who have gone before. They are said to feed among the lilies. For what is meant by lilies but the conduct of those who say with all truth, “We are unto God a sweet savor of Christ.” - "Morals on the Book of Job 24.17"

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: Thy two breasts: Mystically to be understood: the love of God and the love of our neighbour, which are so united as twins which feed among the lilies: that is, the love of God and our neighbour, feeds on the divine mysteries and the holy sacraments, left by Christ to his spouse to feed and nourish her children.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
